title: Graffenrieda scandens
intro: Graffenrieda scandens, also known as "palo amarillo," is a medicinal plant species in the family Melastomataceae. Traditionally, Graffenrieda scandens has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Graffenrieda scandens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Graffenrieda scandens is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies in various forms.
taxonomy: Graffenrieda scandens is classified in the family Melastomataceae. This plant is a member of the order Myrtales and the subclass Rosidae. It belongs to the family Melastomataceae, which is a large family of flowering plants native to the Americas. The class of Graffenrieda scandens is Magnoliopsida, and its phylum is Magnoliophyta.
